codeReview.summary: All 9 required UI components (Button, Card, Badge, Tag, Accordion, Modal, Input, Rating, Carousel) are implemented with proper TypeScript interfaces, cn() utility usage, className prop support, and zero border-radius design system compliance. Components follow named export conventions, are properly organized in src/components/ui/ with a barrel index.ts file. The worker also fixed React 19 lint errors related to ref access during render in Accordion and Carousel components. All validation checks pass (tsc, lint, build). Visual verification confirmed all components render correctly at desktop and mobile widths.

issues.description: The task description mentions 'Input (text, email, textarea with label+error states)' but the Input component only supports <input> elements (extends InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>). There is no textarea variant. However, the canonical features.json description says 'Input (text input with bottom-border style, 0px border-radius)' which does not mention textarea, so this may be intentional. A Textarea component could be added later if needed.

The feature description says 'star rating display with accent color' but Rating uses text-primary (terracotta #C65D3E) instead of text-accent (olive/moss green #6B7F3E). Terracotta is arguably the better visual choice for star ratings — the word 'accent' in the feature description likely means 'brand accent color' (terracotta) rather than the CSS token --color-accent (olive green). This is a reasonable interpretation but worth noting.

Button's as='a' variant renders a raw <a> tag rather than a Next.js Link component. This means anchor-variant buttons won't benefit from client-side navigation. This is acceptable for a general UI component (consumers can wrap with Link), but should be documented for downstream usage.

Accordion content is limited to rendering item.content as a

tag with plain text. If future usage requires rich content (JSX children), the AccordionItemData interface would need content to accept ReactNode instead of string. Current implementation covers the stated requirements but limits extensibility.

CarouselProps.children is typed as React.ReactNode[] (array). This is correct for the SwiperSlide mapping pattern but means a single-child usage would require wrapping in an array. This is a minor DX consideration, not a bug.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker added a UI test page at src/app/ui-test/page.tsx for visual verification (as required by verificationSteps). AGENTS.md does not mention whether test/demo pages should be committed or removed after verification. This test page was committed and will appear in production builds. A convention about temporary verification pages should be documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/app/ui-test/page.tsx committed in c6cc886. AGENTS.md 'Manual Verification' section says to start dev server and verify but doesn't address committing test pages.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md says 'agent-browser viewport limitation: agent-browser always opens at 1280x720 and does not support changing viewport width via flags. Mobile-width (375px) verification must be done via CSS class review.' However, the worker successfully used 'agent-browser set viewport 375 812' to test mobile width, and it worked. The AGENTS.md documentation appears to be outdated or incorrect about agent-browser viewport capabilities.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: 'agent-browser --session ... set viewport 375 812' succeeded and returned a valid mobile screenshot. AGENTS.md states viewport cannot be changed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill procedure was followed correctly. Worker read context files, implemented components, ran tsc/lint/build, fixed lint errors, then verified visually with agent-browser at both desktop and mobile widths. skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true with no deviations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows all 6 procedure steps were followed in order. Handoff skillFeedback confirms followedProcedure: true.

summary: PASS. All 9 UI components are correctly implemented with proper TypeScript typing, cn() utility usage, className support, and zero border-radius compliance. The worker fixed React 19 lint issues proactively and verified all components visually. Five non-blocking issues identified: (1) no textarea variant in Input, (2) Rating uses primary color vs accent token, (3) Button anchor variant uses raw <a> instead of Next.js Link, (4) Accordion content limited to plain text strings, (5) Carousel children typed as array only. None of these block the feature — all stated requirements and expectedBehavior are met. TypeScript, ESLint, and build all pass.
